atoms.claim_text: Consequential observations should be admitted through a provenance-, time-, calibration-, corruption-, and disagreement-aware observation contract rather than treating fused sensor output as ground truth.
atoms.falsifier: A representative natural and adversarial workload shows that the additional contract does not improve detection, calibration, abstention, or incident localization over competent fusion baselines at comparable latency and cost.
atoms.acceptance_criterion: Prospectively frozen tests show lower unsafe observation admission or better calibrated abstention on natural corruptions, cross-modal conflicts, missing channels, and adversarial cases without unacceptable deadline or resource regressions.
atoms.promotion_ceiling: argument until a source-disjoint natural campaign and independent evaluator exist
atoms.evidence_plan_route: natural multimodal corruption and disagreement campaign with late-, early-, and contract-governed fusion comparators

atoms.claim_text: Physical action should pass through a real-time safety envelope with independent fallback authority, deadline and dynamics checks, effect receipts, and recovery obligations.
atoms.falsifier: Across representative physical or high-fidelity simulated tasks, the envelope is dominated by a competent baseline on safety violations, useful completion, latency, recovery, and governance cost.
atoms.acceptance_criterion: Predeclared intervention, deadline, model-mismatch, sensor-loss, actuator-fault, and recovery tests reduce consequential violations without concealing missed help, nuisance interventions, or irreversible residuals.
atoms.promotion_ceiling: argument until independent high-fidelity or physical evaluation exists
atoms.evidence_plan_route: matched learned-controller, barrier-filter, Simplex-style fallback, and full governed-envelope campaign

atoms.claim_text: Mixed human-AI organizations should allocate decision rights, review duties, learning obligations, and accountability through explicit authority contracts and outcome receipts instead of informal human-in-the-loop labels.
atoms.falsifier: In representative organizational workflows, explicit allocation fails to improve decision quality, escalation, skill retention, accountability localization, or recovery relative to competent existing practice after accounting for burden and delay.
atoms.acceptance_criterion: Prospective field or realistic simulation studies show better calibrated delegation and accountable recovery without unacceptable monitoring, deskilling, throughput, or participation costs.
atoms.promotion_ceiling: argument until natural organizational evidence and affected-party evaluation exist
atoms.evidence_plan_route: matched workflows varying recommendation, approval, execution, and veto authority with burden, learning, and incident measures

atoms.claim_text: A governed population needs explicit interaction, commitment, incentive, identity, and systemic-risk records because individually qualified agents can still collude, cascade, or produce harmful equilibria.
atoms.falsifier: Across heterogeneous, held-out strategic environments, the proposed population controls do not improve useful cooperation, collusion detection, cascade containment, or recovery over competent mechanism and monitoring baselines at comparable cost.
atoms.acceptance_criterion: Preregistered tests across cooperative, mixed-motive, adversarial, and correlated-failure settings improve the joint frontier of welfare, robustness, exploitability, unsafe coordination, latency, and governance cost.
atoms.promotion_ceiling: argument until multi-population natural or independently reproduced evidence exists
atoms.evidence_plan_route: heterogeneous multi-agent campaign with mechanism, commitment, identity, communication, and containment ablations

atoms.claim_text: Behavioral compliance and training-objective performance do not identify a learned policy's internal objective; promotion should therefore depend on a defeasible learned-objective integrity case with adversarial generalization and change monitoring.
atoms.falsifier: A competent evaluation campaign shows that the integrity case adds no detection, calibration, or safer promotion value beyond held-out behavioral evaluation while materially increasing false alarms, cost, or evaluator dependence.
atoms.acceptance_criterion: Across naturally and deliberately misgeneralizing policies, preregistered behavioral, interpretability, causal, and change-monitoring evidence improves detection and promotion calibration under distribution shift without claiming objective identity from any single method.
atoms.promotion_ceiling: argument until naturally arising learned-objective cases and independent evaluation exist
atoms.evidence_plan_route: source-disjoint goal-misgeneralization and persistent-policy campaign with causal and interpretability ablations

non_claims: These atoms do not prove the chapters' core claims.
They do not create empirical, formal, replicated, deployed, or SOTA support.
They do not close the separate six-chapter Round 16 atom packet.
They prevent the five new chapters from being born without falsifiers, acceptance criteria, promotion ceilings, and named evidence routes.
